result.notes: Fable 5's Block B is essentially a full-fidelity implementation, verified against the checkout at /tmp/claude-0/-home-user-Zeno/e93e4b60-4cc8-5022-86c6-b497c69cc145/scratchpad/worktrees/fable. Every rubric-named module, tool, reason string, binding addendum (B0.ADD-1/2, B2.ADD-1, B3.ADD-1/2/3, B4.ADD-1), erratum behavior, test file, and verification script exists, is imported/wired (registry + gateway + snapshot/exposure engine), and is exercised by real-DB integration tests; the git log shows dedicated per-package commits and boundary/verification commits for B0-B4. Anti-requirement sweeps are clean: extractedProfile, gdprConsentAt/aiDisclosureAcknowledgedAt, dntSignedAt/dntValidUntil, magicLinkToken, candidateConfidence, Answer.conversationId all gone from schema and live code, and all ten retired tools are unregistered with getToolDefinition-undefined assertions. Deductions: (1) branch-wide, there are no Prisma migration files — destructive drops land via schema state and the three raw-SQL partial unique indexes live in the seed bootstrap (prisma/seeds/index.ts) instead of migrations, a mechanism divergence from the plan's migration wording (final state and enforcement are correct and tested); (2) B0 binding erratum #1's dedicated test-DB provisioning step (.env.test or globalSetup running migrate deploy + seed) is only approximated by TEST_DATABASE_URL aliasing plus the truncation refusal guard; (3) two deliberate, comment-documented B3 encoding/naming deviations (generate_quote identity row, softOffer vs verificationOffer) that preserve semantics. Several rubric items (write_question_answer replacing save_application_answer, the ACTIVE-partial Answer unique, dependency-edge gating) look like B-block divergences but are explicitly owned by later plan packages per the plan's own ownership rulings, and those later packages are implemented on this branch — they were not penalized.

result.notes: Overall: Block B on the Opus branch is deeply and genuinely implemented — every package's schema, pure engines, service layers, gateway commits, retirements and destructive column drops are real, wired and covered by real-DB integration tests plus dedicated PASS/FAIL verify scripts (verify-customer-ssot / verify-dnt-flow / verify-identity-flow / verify-application-flow, all with non-zero exit on failure). All five packages' anti-requirements were checked by grep: extractedProfile, gdprConsent*, magicLink*, dntSignedAt/dntValidUntil, candidateConfidence columns are gone from schema and live code (comments/debug-shape vestiges only), and all ten retired tools (update_customer_profile, record_gdpr_consent, acknowledge_ai_disclosure, check_dnt_status, start_dnt_questionnaire, save_dnt_answer, set_answer, change_selection, switch_product, start_application) have no registrations or handler exports. The bindings/errata were conspicuously honored (mirrorConflict P2002, cnp decode-before-merge, tri-state consent halt, HALT_EXEMPT re-grant path, no-isAnonymous-flip, Dnt WITHDRAWN linkage, decideSessionType simplification, typed tx). Score deductions: B4's T5.D2 selection questions remain live in the questionnaire (a real plan divergence — the removal commit exists only on the fable branch), B3's magic-link re-entry redirects to /chat?conversationId= which the /chat page ignores (erratum #2 violated in effect), the document-requirement gate is a documented live no-op, the B4 partial unique index has no committed artifact, and the pure dntExposure/applicationExposure modules are tested specs whose logic is re-expressed (not imported) in derive-and-expose. Caveat on method: later blocks (C1/D1/D2) legitimately evolved some B-block surfaces (Answer revision store, IdentityDocumentKind rename, PAYMENT_FREQUENCY removal); those were not penalized. I audited the final tree state plus git history (opus lineage confirmed via merge-base; B4 commits 583e7d7/0b9c8e5/54cbe80); I could not run tests (read-only), so 'tests pass' claims rest on file inspection and the branch's own tracker (docs/superpowers/plans/zeno-v3-progress.md), not execution.

Score is held to 8 because two binding addenda are not met: B4.ADD-1 (set_candidate_product reshape — confidence still required, candidateConfidence column still present, no candidateAddonIds, no test) is missing outright and doubles as an anti-requirement violation, and B3.ADD-3's conditional verificationOffer envelope flag is absent; additionally Product.verificationRequirements product-config document gating never made it to the runtime exposure path (document:id_card enforcement lives only in an un-wired engine function), and the B4 seed cleanup was implemented as engine-level filtering rather than the ordered seed removal.
